title: Aspidium moulmaynense
taxonomy: Aspidium moulmaynense is classified as a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair spleenwort family. This genus is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which comprises all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Further classification places Aspidium moulmaynense within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics as a vascular plant that reproduces via spores, setting it apart from flowering plants. By examining the plant's classification, researchers can better understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
